lecture_context.lecture_title: Lecture 17 - ConvNets and TreeRNNs for NLP

unit.name: Deep CNN variants: batch norm, 1x1 conv, and VD-CNN
unit.description: Places CNNs among other sequence models and introduces deeper character-level CNN architectures for text classification.
unit.summary: This segment situates CNNs in the broader modeling toolkit: bag-of-vectors and window models are simple baselines, CNNs are good for classification and easy to parallelize, RNNs are sequential, and transformers have become the dominant NLP model family [ts=1744s][ts=1791s]. The instructor then contrasts batch normalization with layer normalization, explaining that batch norm normalizes across the batch for each feature, whereas layer norm normalizes across the feature dimension [ts=1822s][ts=1888s]. He also explains why a size-1 convolution is meaningful: it acts like a position-wise fully connected layer that re-expresses a single token representation with fewer parameters than a sentence-wide dense layer [ts=1912s][ts=1969s]. The main case study is Conneau et al.'s 2017 very deep CNN for text classification, which starts from characters, stacks deep residual convolutional blocks with local pooling, expands channel depth as sequence length shrinks, and finishes with k-max pooling plus fully connected layers [ts=2013s][ts=2150s][ts=2278s][ts=2338s]. The paper's main result is that deeper models, especially the 29-layer version, performed best and sometimes reached or exceeded prior state of the art on text classification benchmarks [ts=2459s][ts=2520s].

key_points.text: CNNs are easy to parallelize for classification, whereas RNNs process sequences sequentially and transformers now dominate NLP.
Batch normalization normalizes each feature across the batch, unlike layer normalization which normalizes across features.
A size-1 convolution is equivalent to a position-wise fully connected transformation of a token representation.
VD-CNN begins from character inputs and uses deep residual convolutional blocks with repeated local pooling.
The deepest 29-layer VD-CNN variant gave the strongest results in the reported experiments.

kp_catalog.name: Batch normalization and 1x1 convolutions
kp_catalog.description: These CNN building blocks stabilize training and create position-wise feature transformations.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Batch normalization and 1x1 convolutions captures the core idea that These CNN building blocks stabilize training and create position-wise feature transformations.

kp_catalog.name: VD-CNN character-level text classification
kp_catalog.description: A very deep residual CNN starting from characters can perform competitive text classification without pretrained word vectors.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because VD-CNN character-level text classification captures the core idea that A very deep residual CNN starting from characters can perform competitive text classification without pretrained word vectors.

kp_catalog.name: Tree models versus transformers
kp_catalog.description: Tree recursive models may match linguistic structure better in some cases, but transformers win on flexible information flow and overall power.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Tree models versus transformers captures the core idea that Tree recursive models may match linguistic structure better in some cases, but transformers win on flexible information flow and overall power.
